questions.question: Which ranks make up the Noncommissioned Officer (NCO) tier?
options: Staff Sergeant, Technical Sergeant, and Master Sergeant

questions.question: Which skill level is typically earned at the rank of Staff Sergeant?
What PME must Technical Sergeants complete before assuming the grade of Master Sergeant?
Which ranks make up the Senior Noncommissioned Officer (SNCO) tier?
options: Technical Sergeant, Master Sergeant, and Senior Master Sergeant
Master Sergeant, Senior Master Sergeant, and Chief Master Sergeant
Staff Sergeant, Technical Sergeant, and Master Sergeant
Senior Master Sergeant and Chief Master Sergeant

questions.question: What PME must Master Sergeants complete before assuming the grade of Senior Master Sergeant?
Chief Master Sergeants are charged with mentoring and developing junior enlisted personnel and influencing the professional development of which group?
What course must all newly selected RegAF Chief Master Sergeants attend?
The title of Noncommissioned Officer in Charge (NCOIC) is used only for which personnel?
options: NCOs and SNCOs in charge of a work center or element
SNCOs in charge of a flight
Any enlisted member in a leadership position

questions.question: Who was the first Chief Master Sergeant of the Air Force, and when did he assume the unique CMSAF insignia?
options: Chief Paul W. Airey in April 1967
Chief Paul W. Airey in October 1966

questions.question: The CMSAF is the career field manager for which two positions?
options: Command chief master sergeants and group superintendents
Flight chiefs and unit training managers
Senior enlisted leaders and career field managers

questions.question: Enlisted force development combines which three elements in a life-cycle approach to developing Airmen?
The Air Force Enlisted Council is comprised of which group?
options: The current 12 Outstanding Airmen of the Year
Senior enlisted leaders from each COCOM
Selected Chief Master Sergeants from each career field

questions.question: What web-based tool allows individuals greater transparency into their development and career management, and also offers robust mentoring capabilities?
The Occupational Analysis Program is integral to providing an objective orientation for which three force development tenets?
On what typical cycle does Occupational Analysis conduct occupational studies on enlisted USAF specialties?
The USAF on-the-job training (OJT) program consists of which three components?
options: Classroom instruction, field training, and evaluation
Job knowledge, job proficiency, and job experience
Technical training, leadership development, and mentoring
Apprentice training, journeyman training, and craftsman training

questions.question: To be awarded the 7-skill level (Craftsman), the member must be at least what rank?
For award of the 9-skill level (Superintendent), the member must be at least what rank?
Task certifiers must be at least what rank and skill level to provide third-party certification?
In 1955, enlisted PME schools were established coinciding with what other significant event?
options: The creation of the CMSAF position
The founding of the Community College of the Air Force
The release of the first official enlisted force structure
The establishment of Air Education and Training Command

questions.question: The Chief Leadership Course (CLC) educates approximately how many Total Force Chief Master Sergeants and Chief Master Sergeant-selects annually?
When was the Community College of the Air Force (CCAF) activated?
What is the total semester-hour requirement for the typical CCAF associate of applied science degree?
The AFCOOL program funds a maximum of how much during an Airman's career for specialty-applicable credentials?
EJPME consists of two courses of instruction. EJPME I emphasizes curriculum commensurate with which grade levels' joint assignment responsibilities?
What is the written abbreviation for Staff Sergeant, and what are the official terms of address?
What is the duty title of Section Chief used for?
options: Any SNCO managing a program at USAF level
NCOs and SNCOs in charge of a flight
NCOs and SNCOs in charge of a section with at least two subordinate work centers or elements
Chief Master Sergeants who are program managers at designated USAF levels

questions.question: In 1966, which Congressman introduced a bill that would mandate each service to appoint one senior enlisted member?
Which Chief of Staff of the Air Force announced the newly created position of CMSAF in October 1966?
The command chief master sergeant (CCM) traditionally serves at which designated USAF levels?
options: Wings, centers, numbered air forces (NAF), and major commands (MAJCOM)
Combatant commands and joint task forces only

questions.question: The title of Senior Enlisted Leader (SEL) is used for SNCOs in charge at which organizational levels?
options: COCOM and joint task force levels

questions.question: The Enlisted Force Development Panel includes command chief master sergeants, career field managers, and representatives from which organizations?
options: Air Combat Command, Air Mobility Command, and Pacific Air Forces
Air Education and Training Command, Air Force Personnel Center, and HAF
Defense Language Institute, Air University, and DANTES
Office of the Secretary of Defense, Joint Staff, and Congress

questions.question: What feature does MyVECTOR offer that provides confidential feedback from supervisors, subordinates, peers, or higher-ranking members?
Within how many days of assignment must newly assigned personnel be interviewed to determine training status?
options: 14 days (30 days for Air Reserve Component)
30 days (60 days for Air Reserve Component)
60 days (90 days for Air Reserve Component)
90 days (120 days for Air Reserve Component)

questions.question: At what month of upgrade training must a training progress review be conducted with the unit training manager, supervisor, and trainee?
The SNCO Academy (SNCOA) trains up to how many USAF, Navy, Coast Guard, Marine, and international SNCOs annually?
Which President signed Public Law 94-361 on 14 July 1976, authorizing the Commander of Air Training Command to confer the associate of applied science degree?
CCAF offers associate of applied science degree programs in which four broad career groups?
The Montgomery GI Bill requires participants to have their pay reduced by how much per month for 12 months?
The Inter-American Air Forces Academy (IAAFA) was initially established as a training program on 15 March 1943 at whose request?
options: Peru's Minister of Aeronautics, General Fernando Melgar
Mexico's Chief of the Air Force
